headline: Solstice, Element Solutions weigh $27 billion merger, FT reports
summary: Honeywell spinoff Solstice Advanced Materials is in talks to merge with Element Solutions in a deal ‌that could create a chemicals company valued at about $27 ‌billion including debt, the Financial Times reported on Monday.  The talks happen as both ​companies seek to capitalize on growing demand for specialty chemicals used in AI data centers and semiconductor manufacturing.  Solstice and Element Solutions are discussing a merger of equals and a deal, likely to be ‌mostly stock with ⁠some cash, could come together as soon as this week, the FT report said, citing people familiar ⁠with the talks.

headline: Honeywell spinoff Solstice to buy Element Solutions for $14.5 billion
summary: Honeywell spinoff Solstice Advanced Materials said on Monday it will buy Element Solutions at ‌about $14.5 billion, including debt, in a cash-and-stock deal. The ‌deal comes less than a year after Solstice completed its spin-off from ​industrial conglomerate Honeywell International. The combination would create a larger supplier of materials used in semiconductor manufacturing, electronics and industrial applications.

headline: Honeywell (HON) Stock Looks Fairly Valued After Its 46% Fall
summary: Honeywell International stock has fallen sharply over the past month, yet the market-multiple checks still screen it as undervalued, while the broader valuation score suggests the shares are not an obvious bargain. Over the past month, Honeywell International is down 46.3%, which puts the recent sell off at the center of any valuation discussion. The push into AI enabled industrial software, highlighted by launches like Experion Cognition and research with MIT on digital energy efficiency,...

headline: Honeywell (HON) Launches Experion Cognition As AI Becomes Central To Its New Focus
summary: Honeywell International (NasdaqGS:HON) has launched Experion Cognition, an AI-powered autonomous control system for industrial customers. The launch comes one week after Honeywell completed its post spin transformation into an independent automation focused business. Experion Cognition targets process, petrochemical, and building automation markets by combining AI with Honeywell’s established process control technology. Honeywell International is using its newly focused automation profile...

headline: Blockbuster AI Deal Shows Why Honeywell Had to Break Up
summary: Solstice Advanced Materials  isn’t waiting around to create a legacy after being spun off from  Honeywell  in October, 2025.  Monday morning, the maker of products for advanced cooling solutions and nuclear power said it was buying  Element Solutions  for $14.5 billion.  Element shareholders will get 0.5 Solstice share and $10 in cash per Element share held.

content.title: Blockbuster AI Deal Shows Why Honeywell Had to Break Up
content.summary: Solstice Advanced Materials  isn’t waiting around to create a legacy after being spun off from  Honeywell  in October, 2025. Element shareholders will get 0.5 Solstice share and $10 in cash per Element share held.

headline: Honeywell Spinoff Solstice Inks $14.5 Billion Deal For 'Increased Exposure' To AI Boom
summary: Solstice Advanced Materials sees Element Solutions giving it "increased exposure" to the AI boom. The Honeywell spinoff tumbled on the news.

headline: Solstice buys Element Solutions in $14.5 billion deal, sharpens focus on AI market
summary: Honeywell spinoff Solstice Advanced Materials said it will buy peer specialty chemical company Element Solutions in a $14.5 billion deal, ‌as it seeks to capitalize on growing demand from AI data ‌centers and the chip industries.  The deal would create a larger supplier of materials used in semiconductor ​manufacturing, electronics and industrial applications, combining Solstice's refrigerants, specialty materials and uranium-conversion business with Element's electronics chemicals portfolio.  Shares of Solstice dropped nearly 13%, while those of Element fell 2.1% on Monday, following the announcement of the cash-and-stock deal.

content.title: Solstice buys Element Solutions in $14.5 billion deal, sharpens focus on AI market
content.summary: Honeywell spinoff Solstice Advanced Materials said it will buy peer specialty chemical company Element Solutions in a $14.5 billion deal, ‌as it seeks to capitalize on growing demand from AI data ‌centers and the chip industries.  The deal would create a larger supplier of materials used in semiconductor ​manufacturing, electronics and industrial applications, combining Solstice's refrigerants, specialty materials and uranium-conversion business with Element's electronics chemicals portfolio.  Shares of Solstice dropped nearly 13%, while those of Element fell 2.1% on Monday, following the announcement of the cash-and-stock deal.
